nodes.name: HTTP: HN Algolia search
nodes.notes: design.md §2.1 live measurement: nbHits=1380 over a 14-day window, hitsPerPage=1000 is Algolia's own page ceiling (deeper needs time-slicing, not built here). Keyless.

nodes.name: Loop candidates (Split in Batches)
nodes.notes: outputs: [done, loop] (n8n-nodes-base SplitInBatchesV3 source, confirmed live in the running container). 'done' (index 0) fires once, carrying the concatenation of every loop-body iteration's output -- fed straight into 'Run counters -> events' below. 'loop' (index 1) is the per-candidate body.

nodes.name: HTTP: HN item tree
nodes.notes: design.md §2.1: median num_comments is 0 -- an absent thread is a `missing` claim, not a failure; it is the common case.

nodes.name: HTTP: HN user karma
nodes.notes: $json here is the PREVIOUS node's (HTTP: HN item tree) raw response -- HN's /items/{id} shape carries `author` at the top level, same person as hnStory.author.

nodes.name: Tier 1 - create entities + raw signals
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f02/{normalize,identity,claims,obscurity,pipeline,write}.js -- do not edit here, edit there and re-run n8n/build-f02-workflow.py.

nodes.name: IF: opted out?
nodes.notes: design.md §7 item 2: opt-out is enforced AT INGEST by write.js's applyWriteSet (lib/f02/ethics.js's isOptedOut), before Tier 1 writes anything. A blocked candidate has no application_id -- routes straight to the loop-back, never to the gate.

nodes.name: Gate: f07-thesis-gate
nodes.notes: mode:'keyword' -- no LLM, no GitHub token. Flat payload, no .body wrapper (design.md §5.5(c)). Sub-workflow output REPLACES $json with the gate's return contract: {application_id, thesis_id, evaluation_id, mode, verdict, fit, coverage, fired_rules, missing_fields}.

nodes.name: IF: verdict != failed
nodes.notes: design.md §5.5(c): FOUR-way outcome (passed | borderline | insufficient_evidence (response) == NULL (column) | failed) collapses to a two-way branch here -- only 'failed' stops. keyword mode never returns 'passed' by design, so advancing only on 'passed' would advance nobody.

nodes.name: Merge Tier 2 branches
nodes.notes: mode:'append', numberInputs:2 -- GitHub branch -> input 0, Tavily branch -> input 1. The downstream node ignores this node's own output payload (it pulls GitHub/Tavily data via named-node lookups instead); this Merge exists purely as the synchronization gate that guarantees BOTH branches completed before Tier 2's write runs -- see TRACKER.md ~05:05.

nodes.name: Tier 2 - Build write set + persist
nodes.notes: SOURCE OF TRUTH: lib/f02/{normalize,identity,claims,obscurity,pipeline,write}.js -- do not edit here, edit there and re-run n8n/build-f02-workflow.py. Second write pass, full capabilities -- see the in-node comment on the documented duplicate-applications-row limitation.

parameters.content:
### TIER 0 -- free, deterministic, no LLM, no key
HN Algolia search_by_date, 14-day window, points>=2. Drop hits with no
url; sort by created_at_i DESC; take gate_budget (default 120).
The cap is by RECENCY, never by points (design.md §2/§10 decision 2).
Dropped counts are always logged into counters0, never silent.

### TIER 1 -- one extra call per survivor, then GATE
HN comment tree + user karma. Then founders + companies +
applications are created BEFORE any raw_signals row is written
(design.md §5.0 rule 0) -- a raw_signals row with both FKs NULL can
never be reached by purge_founder() and the table is append-only.

GATE calls f07-thesis-gate in mode:'keyword' (no LLM, no GitHub
token). FOUR-way outcome collapses to a two-way branch: only
'failed' stops (design.md §5.5(c)) -- keyword mode never returns
'passed' by design, so gating on 'passed' would advance nobody.

### TIER 2 -- expensive, only past the gate
GitHub REST (unauthenticated, 60 req/h -- no GITHUB_TOKEN configured,
design.md §11) and Tavily /map -> one batched /extract run as
genuinely PARALLEL branches and reconverge through a real Merge node
(mode:'append', numberInputs:2) -- TRACKER.md ~05:05: several wires
into one plain node's input does NOT reliably wait for both branches
in this n8n build. Tavily enforces a hard per-run credit ceiling via
workflow static data (design.md §7.2, default 150).

"Tier 2 - Build write set + persist" calls buildWriteSet then the
writer a SECOND time (full capabilities) -- see its own in-node
comment for the documented duplicate-applications-row limitation.

### LOOP: Split in Batches
batchSize:1. 'loop' output (index 1) feeds the per-candidate chain;
BOTH exit paths ("Tier 2 - Build write set + persist" success and
"Rejected - skip Tier 2") wire back into this node's single input --
an exclusive IF/Switch reconverge is fine with multiple wires (only
one branch is ever live per candidate); ONLY the genuinely parallel
GitHub/Tavily fan-in above needs the real Merge node.

'done' output (index 0) fires once, carrying every candidate's
summary item concatenated -> "Run counters -> events".
